title: Quiz: Communication Efficiency
description: Test your understanding of communication-efficient federated learning

questions.question: Why is communication efficiency important in federated learning?

options.text: Bandwidth is often limited and transmitting parameters is costly

questions.explanation: In federated learning, clients must transmit model updates each round, which can be bandwidth-limited and costly.

questions.question: What does top-k gradient sparsification do?

options.text: Keeps only the k largest gradient values by magnitude

questions.explanation: Top-k sparsification transmits only the k largest gradient values (by absolute value), reducing communication.

questions.question: What is 'error feedback' in gradient compression?

options.text: Accumulating compression residuals for the next round
Sending error messages to the server

questions.explanation: Error feedback accumulates the values not transmitted (residuals) and adds them to the next round's gradient.

questions.question: Stochastic quantization is unbiased in expectation.
questions.explanation: True. Stochastic quantization rounds up or down with probability proportional to the fractional part, making it unbiased.

questions.question: What is the trade-off of using fewer bits for quantization?

options.text: No trade-off, fewer bits is always better
Lower communication but higher quantization error

questions.explanation: Fewer bits reduce communication but introduce quantization error, which can affect convergence.

questions.question: What is Local SGD?

options.text: Performing multiple local updates before synchronizing
Using local time instead of global time

questions.explanation: Local SGD accumulates multiple local gradient steps before communicating, reducing synchronization frequency.

questions.question: What happens if sync frequency is too low in Local SGD?

options.text: Clients may drift and convergence may slow

questions.explanation: With infrequent synchronization, client models can diverge (drift), potentially slowing convergence or hurting final accuracy.

questions.question: Which techniques reduce communication in federated learning? (Select all)

options.text: Sending raw data instead of gradients

questions.explanation: Sparsification, quantization, and local accumulation all reduce communication. Sending raw data would increase it.
